title: Maximum Sum Subarray of Size K
description: Sharechat tracks per-minute engagement counts for a viral post. Given an array `views` of integers and an integer `k`, return the maximum sum of any contiguous window of exactly `k` minutes. If `k > views.length`, return 0.

examples.explanation: Window [9,2,6] sums to 17, the highest 3-window sum.

hints: Pehle k elements ka sum nikal le, fir window slide kar
Slide karte time naya add, purana minus
Edge: k > n hua to 0 return kar

title: Number of Sub-arrays of Size K With Average Below Threshold
description: Zomato batches consecutive orders to assign a rider. Given delivery times `times` (minutes) and `k`, return the count of contiguous batches of size `k` whose average time is strictly less than a given `threshold`. If `k > times.length`, return 0.
hints: Avg compare karne ke bajaye sum vs k*threshold compare kar
Window sum slide kar k size ka
Strictly less than condition mein equal count nahi hota

title: Window Sum Exceeds Limit
description: PhonePe risk team flags any user whose `k`-transaction window total ever crosses `limit` rupees. Given amounts array `txns` and integers `k`, `limit`, return `true` if any window of size `k` has sum greater than `limit`, else `false`. If `k > txns.length`, return `false`.
hints: Standard fixed window — bas early return kar de jab cross ho
Strict greater than dhyan se
k zyada ho to false

title: Maximum Number of Vowels in a Substring of Given Length
description: BookMyShow generates promo codes from movie names. They want the maximum number of vowels (`a, e, i, o, u`) that appear in any contiguous substring of length `k` from a lowercase string `s`. If `k > s.length`, return 0.
hints: Vowel set use kar fast lookup ke liye
Window slide kar — entering vowel +1, leaving vowel -1
Initial window pehle compute karna na bhul

title: Minimum Sum Subarray of Size K
description: A Swiggy rider works `k` consecutive hours per shift. Given an hourly order-count array `loads`, return the minimum possible total orders for any contiguous shift of length `k`. If `k > loads.length`, return -1.
hints: Max ki jagah min track kar
Standard fixed window template
k > n hua to -1

title: Max Consecutive Ones
description: Razorpay analytics team wants the longest streak of consecutive successful transactions. Given an array `status` of 0s and 1s (1 = success), return the length of the longest contiguous subarray of all 1s.
hints: Variable window — left fix, right expand jab tak 1 mile
0 dikha to left = right + 1 kar de
Empty array case sambhal

title: Distinct Elements in First Window
description: Meesho recommendation engine looks at the last `k` product IDs viewed. Given array `views` (product IDs) and integer `k`, return the count of distinct products in the FIRST window of size exactly `k`. If `k > views.length`, return 0.
hints: Sirf first window — Set use kar
k > n ho to 0
Set ka size return kar

title: Fixed-Size Window Sums
description: Cred shows users a moving average of reward points earned per day. Given `points` (daily points) and `k` (window size), return an array where the i-th element (0-indexed) is the SUM of points from day i to day i+k-1. The output length is `n - k + 1`. If `k > n`, return `[]`.
hints: Sare windows ke sums chahiye — array banao
Slide kar O(n) mein
k > n hua to khali array

title: Minimum Window Substring
description: Uber dispatcher gets a stream of ride requests as a string `requests`, each character a city code. Given a string `needed` of city codes that must all be covered (with multiplicity), return the length of the smallest contiguous substring of `requests` containing every required code at least as many times as in `needed`. If no such window exists, return 0.
hints: Variable window: right expand, fir left contract jab valid ho
HashMap mein needed counts rakh, fir matched counter maintain kar
Min length update karte raho jab valid ho

title: Longest Substring with At Most K Distinct Characters
description: Atlassian's editor logs keystrokes as a string `keys`. Find the length of the longest contiguous substring containing at most `k` distinct characters. Useful for shortcut-pattern detection.
hints: Variable window with HashMap of char freq
Distinct count > k ho to left contract kar
Empty string ya k=0 edge case

title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
description: Flipkart wants to find the longest streak of recent SKU views with NO duplicate SKU. Given an array `skus` of integers, return the length of the longest contiguous subarray containing only unique values.
hints: HashMap me last seen index store kar
Duplicate dikhne par left ko Math.max(left, lastSeen+1) pe le ja
Empty array case

title: Sliding Window Maximum
description: Paytm dashboard shows the maximum settlement value over every rolling `k`-day window. Given integer array `nums` and integer `k`, return an array where the i-th element is the max of nums[i..i+k-1]. Output length is `n - k + 1`. If `k > n`, return `[]`.
hints: Monotonic decreasing deque of indices use kar
Front index window se bahar nikla to pop
Naya element add karne se pehle peeche se chote saare nikal

title: Sliding Window Minimum
description: Google Ads system reports the lowest CPC in every rolling window of `k` consecutive auctions. Given array `cpc` and integer `k`, return an array of mins of each contiguous `k`-length window. If `k > n`, return `[]`.
hints: Monotonic INCREASING deque
Same logic as max but reverse comparison
Front always points to current min

title: Find All Anagrams in a String
description: Amazon search uses anagram matching for typo-tolerant queries. Given two lowercase strings `text` and `pat`, return the count of substrings in `text` that are anagrams of `pat`.
hints: Fixed window length = pat.length
Char freq array of size 26
Compare arrays cleverly using matched counter

title: Max Consecutive Ones III
description: Postman load balancer logs each request as success (1) or failure (0). You may flip at most `k` failures to successes. Return the length of the longest contiguous run of 1s achievable after at most `k` flips.

examples.explanation: Flip 2 zeros in the right stretch to get 7 consecutive 1s.

hints: Variable window: zeros count <= k allowed
Right expand, left contract jab zeros > k
Best window length track kar

title: Subarray Product Less Than K
description: Microsoft Azure billing analyzer wants the count of contiguous subarrays of positive integers `costs` whose product is strictly less than `k`. Return that count.
hints: Variable window with running product
Product >= k ho to left badha aur divide kar
Har valid right pe (r - l + 1) subarrays add ho

title: Minimum Size Subarray Sum
description: Swiggy needs the smallest contiguous window of positive integer order-values `orders` whose sum is at least `S`. Return that window length, or 0 if no such window exists.
hints: Variable window: expand jab sum < S, contract jab sum >= S
Min length record karte raho
Positive only — monotonic

title: Longest Repeating Character Replacement
description: Zomato testing infra checks log compression. Given an uppercase string `log` and integer `k`, you may replace at most `k` characters with any uppercase letter. Return the length of the longest substring achievable that consists of a single repeating character.

examples.explanation: Replace one B → 'AABA'+'A' chunk yields 'AAAA' window length 4.

hints: Window mein max-freq char track kar
Window length - maxFreq <= k valid hai
Violation ho to left aage

title: Count Requests in Time Window
description: Razorpay throttles API by counting requests in the last `windowMs` milliseconds. Given a sorted (ascending) array `timestamps` of request times in ms and a window size `windowMs`, return an array `counts` where `counts[i]` is the number of requests in the half-open window `(timestamps[i] - windowMs, timestamps[i]]`.

examples.explanation: For t=10, window is (5,10], includes 10 — count 1. Wait recompute: t=10, window (5,10] includes only 10 → 1. Actual answer differs. Recompute strictly: t=1 (-4,1] → {1}=1. t=2 (-3,2] → {1,2}=2. t=5 (0,5] → {1,2,5}=3. t=10 (5,10] → {10}=1. t=11 (6,11] → {10,11}=2. So output is [1,2,3,1,2].

hints: Two pointers — left aage badha jab tak ts[left] <= ts[r] - windowMs
Half-open window (ts[r] - windowMs, ts[r]]
Counts are r - left + 1

title: Count Distinct Elements in Every Window of Size K
description: PhonePe analytics needs the distinct merchant count for every contiguous window of size `k` in transaction stream `merchants`. Return an array of distinct counts, one per window, length `n - k + 1`. If `k > n`, return `[]`.
hints: Frequency map maintain kar
Add new element, remove leftmost when window size > k
Distinct count = map.size jab freq>0

title: Longest Window Containing All Values
description: Uber rates trips on a 1–5 scale. Given `ratings` array, return the length of the longest contiguous window in which EVERY rating between 1 and 5 (inclusive) appears at least once. If no such window exists, return 0.

examples.explanation: Smallest cover is [1..5], the only valid window length is 5.

hints: Find SMALLEST window covering 1-5, then... wait, problem says LONGEST
Longest window covering all 5 — basically suffix-prefix
Track first occurrence and last occurrence?

title: Subarrays with K Different Integers
description: Amazon shopper-segmentation needs the count of contiguous subarrays of `events` that contain exactly `k` distinct event types. Return that count.
hints: Trick: exactly K = atMost(K) - atMost(K-1)
atMost(K) classic sliding window pattern
Cumulative count ko subarray-ending-at-r se add kar

title: Minimum Window Substring II
description: Google search ranks pages by smallest token windows. Given two strings `s` and `t` over lowercase letters, return the smallest substring of `s` that contains every character of `t` (with multiplicity). If multiple smallest, return the LEFTMOST. If none exists, return empty string.

examples.explanation: 'banc' is the leftmost smallest containing a, b, c.

hints: Need-freq map se start kar
Window expand, fir contract jab valid
Best (left, right) update karo only if strictly smaller — leftmost banaye rakhne ke liye

title: Contiguous Array
description: BookMyShow analytics: each seat is either booked (1) or empty (0). Given binary array `seats`, return the length of the longest contiguous window with EQUAL number of booked and empty seats.
hints: 0 ko -1 treat kar, fir prefix sum 0 wala longest subarray dhundh
Pehli baar dikhne wala prefix sum index store kar
Pure sliding window nahi — prefix sum + hashmap (extended sliding window pattern)

title: Subarray Sum Equals K
description: Swiggy growth team counts how many contiguous windows of integer engagement scores have sum exactly equal to a given target `T`. Return the count of contiguous subarrays of `scores` (which may include negatives) whose sum is `T`.
hints: Negatives hain to pure sliding window nahi — prefix sum + hashmap
Har prefix sum ki freq store kar
Current prefix sum - T pehle aaya tha to count += freq
